abstract: The angle in degrees where the spotlight is cut off.
overview:
## Discussion

If the `w` component of the position is not equal to `0.0`, the value of the [doc://com.apple.glkit/documentation/GLKit/GLKEffectPropertyLight/spotCutoff] property determines whether the light is a point light or a spotlight. A cutoff value of `180.0` indicates that the light is a point light; for a point light, the [doc://com.apple.glkit/documentation/GLKit/GLKEffectPropertyLight/spotDirection] and [doc://com.apple.glkit/documentation/GLKit/GLKEffectPropertyLight/spotExponent] properties are ignored. Otherwise, the [doc://com.apple.glkit/documentation/GLKit/GLKEffectPropertyLight/spotCutoff] property represents the maximum angle at which the light contributes lighting to the scene. The angle is measured between the vector provided by the [doc://com.apple.glkit/documentation/GLKit/GLKEffectPropertyLight/spotDirection] property and a vector drawn from the light’s position to the point being lit. If the angle exceeds this amount, then the light contributes no light to the scene.

The default value is `180.0`.

# spotCutoff

**Instance Property**

The angle in degrees where the spotlight is cut off.

## Declaration

```swift
var spotCutoff: GLfloat { get set }
```

## Discussion

If the `w` component of the position is not equal to `0.0`, the value of the [doc://com.apple.glkit/documentation/GLKit/GLKEffectPropertyLight/spotCutoff] property determines whether the light is a point light or a spotlight. A cutoff value of `180.0` indicates that the light is a point light; for a point light, the [doc://com.apple.glkit/documentation/GLKit/GLKEffectPropertyLight/spotDirection] and [doc://com.apple.glkit/documentation/GLKit/GLKEffectPropertyLight/spotExponent] properties are ignored. Otherwise, the [doc://com.apple.glkit/documentation/GLKit/GLKEffectPropertyLight/spotCutoff] property represents the maximum angle at which the light contributes lighting to the scene. The angle is measured between the vector provided by the [doc://com.apple.glkit/documentation/GLKit/GLKEffectPropertyLight/spotDirection] property and a vector drawn from the light’s position to the point being lit. If the angle exceeds this amount, then the light contributes no light to the scene.

The default value is `180.0`.

## Configuring Spotlight Properties

- **spotDirection**: A vector indicating the direction the spotlight is projecting.
- **spotExponent**: A value indicating how focused the spotlight is.
